parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your class has attended a seminar about how people could be encouraged to buy fewer things and value them more. You have made the notes below. Write an essay discussing two of the ideas in your notes. You should explain which idea would be more effective, giving reasons to support your opinion.

stimulus.title: How could people be encouraged to buy fewer things and value them more?
stimulus.body:
• making repair cheaper and easier than replacement
• requiring advertising to be honest about how products are made and how long they last
• making second-hand goods fashionable rather than second-best

Some opinions expressed in the seminar:
"We threw away the repair shops, then wondered why we throw away everything else."
"An advert may not lie outright, but it is never in the business of telling the truth."
"The moment vintage became stylish, a million wardrobes stopped being rubbish."

bullets: explain which would be more effective
give reasons to support your opinion

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: An English-language lifestyle website has invited reviews of second-hand shopping experiences.

stimulus.title: Second-hand, first-rate?
stimulus.body: Review a second-hand shop, market or resale app you have used. Describe what it offers and what finding something good there actually involves, compare the experience with buying new, and say honestly who it will and will not suit.

bullets: what it offers and what finding something good involves
how the experience compares with buying new
who it will and will not suit

tasks.lead: Your town council has invited proposals for projects to reduce household waste.

stimulus.title: Proposal: a monthly repair café
stimulus.body: Write a proposal for a monthly event at which volunteers help residents repair clothes, furniture and electronics instead of discarding them. Explain how the café would be staffed and equipped, how you would persuade people that repaired goods are not inferior, and what the town would gain beyond less waste.

bullets: how the café would be staffed and equipped
how you would change attitudes to repaired goods
what the town would gain beyond less waste

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Toni.

stimulus.body:
From: Toni
Subject: Dream job... I think?

I've been offered a job managing social media influencers for a big fashion brand. Great salary, exciting industry - but the work is basically getting people to present adverts as if they were honest personal recommendations. A year ago I wrote my dissertation criticising exactly this!

Am I overthinking it? You always see these things clearly - what would you do?

Toni

bullets: whether Toni is overthinking the ethical problem
your honest advice on taking the job
how Toni could act on their principles, whichever choice they make
